c1.speaker: narrator
c1.line: Ledger open, visor down. Ida taps a fifty-year-old entry with one tiny finger.

c2.speaker: ida
c2.line: Fifty years ago, two keys, my own apprentice hands. One to the council, one to Edmund. No copies since — this lock would tell me if a copy had ever been tried.

c3.speaker: ida
c3.line: Locks are terrible gossips, Mr. Gibbons. This one's only ever known its two.
